name: Belgium
contextBlurb:
As of 2025, Belgium regulates stablecoins primarily through the EU Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA). MiCA mandates authorisation for issuers, full-reserve backing and transparency/audit rules for EMTs/ARTs. CASPs active before 30 Dec 2024 may continue under the EU transitional regime until 1 Jul 2026, and MiCA-authorised firms can serve Belgium via passporting.

Belgium also restricts retail distribution of certain derivatives (e.g., CFDs), which covers crypto-CFDs. A consortium of European banks (including Belgium’s KBC) has announced plans for a MiCA-compliant euro stablecoin targeted for 2H 2026.

legislation.title: Apr 2026 ESMA confirmed that the MiCA transitional period ends mid-2026; crypto firms must hold a full MiCA/CASP licence by that deadline. As of April 2026, 199 CASPs are licensed across the EU; no Asset-Referenced Token (ART) issuer has yet been approved.
Nine European banks (incl. KBC) announce plan for a MiCA-compliant euro stablecoin targeted for H2 2026.
Belgium announces a 10% capital-gains tax on financial assets (incl. crypto) effective Jan 1, 2026.
CASP regime applies; FSMA confirms transitional period until Jul 1, 2026 for existing providers.
MiCA stablecoin rules (Titles III & IV) start applying EU-wide (incl. Belgium).

keyFigures.name: Financial Services and Markets Authority (FSMA)
keyFigures.role: Belgium’s MiCA competent authority; licenses/supervises ART/EMT issuers (non-banks) and CASPs; marketing & conduct rules

keyFigures.name: National Bank of Belgium (NBB)
keyFigures.role: Macro/financial-stability oversight (incl. crypto risks) and cooperation with FSMA; prudential supervisor of Belgian ban

news.headline: FSMA - Crypto Asset Service Provider (CASP): MiCA passport & transitional period (to 1 Jul 2026)
Digital Watch – ESMA confirms end of MiCA transition period (April 2026)

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn:
As of 2025, Belgium regulates stablecoins primarily through the EU Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA). MiCA mandates authorisation for issuers, full-reserve backing and transparency/audit rules for EMTs/ARTs. CASPs active before 30 Dec 2024 may continue under the EU transitional regime until 1 Jul 2026, and MiCA-authorised firms can serve Belgium via passporting.

Belgium also restricts retail distribution of certain derivatives (e.g., CFDs), which covers crypto-CFDs. A consortium of European banks (including Belgium’s KBC) has announced plans for a MiCA-compliant euro stablecoin targeted for 2H 2026.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote:
Belgium applies EU MiCA: stablecoins are Electronic Money Tokens (EMTs) or Asset-Referenced Tokens (ARTs).
EMTs are single-fiat and fully backed; reserves must include ≥30% bank deposits (≥60% if “significant”).
